content.full_text:
California
Fair P01itical
Practices Cornrnission
March 22, 199 0
Tan Koo
PubIic Adninistrator-coroner Business l'tanager
office of Public Adroinistrator
760 West Ni.elsen Avenue
Fresno, CA 937 06
Re: Your Reguest for Informal Assistance
our File No. I-9 o-07 2
Dear l,ls . Koo:
You have requested advice concerning the new disclosure
reouirements under Section 87200 of the Political Reform Act (the
,46t")1 as they pertain to the duties of the Fresno county Pubtic
Adninistrator-Coroner and the Public Administrator-Coroner Busi-
ness Manager.
The conmission staff is in the process of draftinq regula-
tions to implement Section 872OO, with the intent of presenting
theu for adoption by the commission in Ju1y. rt is possible that
the coEmission :uay idopt regulations which roay be at variance with
the advice in-this letter. 
we advise that you contact us
regarding the status of these regu).ations as they pertain to.your
paiticulir 
situation. 
rn the meantirne, we can provide you with
Lhe following guidance, but $re consider this reply to be infornal
assi,stance pursuant to Regulation 18329(c) (copy enclosed) .'
OUESTION
The Fresno county Public Adninistrator-coroner and the busi-
ness manager deposit decedent's individual, estate funds into
insured accounts at financial institutions. 
Under the Act, are
1 Gor".rrr"nt Code Sections 81OOO-91015. A11 statutory references
are to the Governnent Code unless otherrrise indicated. Commission
regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations Section
18000, et seq. AI1 references to regulations are to Title 2,
Division 6 of the CaLifornia Code of Regulations.
2 Inforsral assistance does not provide the requestor t ith the
inrnunity provided by an opinion or formal written advice. (Govern-
nent Code Section 83114; 2 CaL. code of Regs. Section
1832e (c) (3) . )
428 J Street, Suite 8OO . P,O. Box 8OZ o Sacramento CA 959O4-O8O? . (916)322.5660


File No. l-9O-O72
Page 2
the County PubIic Adninistrator-coroner and the business manager
managing the investment of public funds, which would make them
required filers under Section 87200?
CONCLUSION
The Fresno County Public Adninistrator-coroner and the busi-
ness Danager do not nanage public investments and are not subject
to the new disclosure requirements under Section a72oo.
FACTS
David M. Hadden, IiI.D. is the Fresno County Public
Adrninistrator-Coroner. He is an elected official, 
tho has a duty
to deposit estate funds pursuant to Probate code Section 7640. In
your capacity as the Public Adninistrator-coroner Business
lrlanager, you deposit the estate funds under the supervision of Dr.
Hadden. Probate Code Section 7640 states that:
(a) The public adninistrator shall, upon
receipt, deposit a1t noney of the estate in an
insured account in a financial institution or with
the county treasurer of the county in which the
proceedings are pending.
(b) Upon deposit under this section the
public administrator is discharged fron further
responsibility for the money deposited until the
public adruinistrator $rithdraws the Doney.
Therefore, if the Public Adninistrator-coroner does not
deposit all money of the private individual decedent's estate with
the county treasurer, he or she has a duty to deposit this noney
into an insured account in a bank or savings and loan institution.
The financial institutions treat these deposits as private and
individual deposits; they are not classified as public funds which
reguire a special collateral insurance provision. The deposits
are linked to each individual estate and only escheat to the state
if there are no beneficiaries or other persons who are entitled to
the money or who clairn the noney.
ANALYSIS
Section 872Oo, et seo., provides that certain specified
elected and appointed public officials, 
and candidates for these
positions, uust file stateEents of econonic interest. 
Effective
January 1, 1990, Assenbly BilI 941 (Lernpert) anended Section 87200
to include city and county treasurers, and all other public of-
ficials and enployees who nanage the investment of putlic funds,
and candidates for any of those offices which are elective. 
Sec-
tion 87200 now states that:
This article is applicable to elected state
officers, judges and commissioners of courts of the


File No. 
■-90-072
Page 3
judicial branch of government, rnembers of the
Public Utilities 
Connission, Derobers of the state
Energy Resources Conservation and DeveloPment Com-
mission, neubers of the Fair Political Practices
Coromission, Dembers of the california coastal com-
Dission, Denbers of planning commissions, :nentbers
of the board of supervisors, district attorneys,
county counsels, county treasurers, and chief
admj,nistrative officers of counties, rnayors, city
managers, city attorneys, city treasurers, chief
adninistrative officers and mernbers of city
councils of cities, and other public officials 
who
manage public investments, and to candidates for
any of these offices at any election.
Prior to the anendment of Section 87200, city and county
treasurers and other public officials 
$rho manage public invest-
uents were required to fife a ForE 730 financial disclosure state-
nent only if €hey were rrdesignatedrr in their agency's conflict-of-
interest code. since that created widely disparate disclosure
requireroents, the Comnission sought to change this by requirinq
uniforro disclosure by public officials 
who make decisions that
affect taxpayer funds.
The new requirement to file statements of economic interests
(Forn 721) pursuant to section 87200 includes:
(1) AII city and countY treasurers,
(2)- A11 other public officials and employees who nanage
public investments, including, but not lirnited to employees
and consultants who have decision Daking authority with
regard to investnent of city, county or state funds. This
includes, but is not linited to, pension fund managers for
public agencies, as well as any person to whou a city or
county treasurer has delegated such authority; and
(3) A11 candidates for any of the above offices which
are electi,ve.
We construe the tern ttpublic investnentl to include invest-
uents uade with pubJ.ic noneys. ttPublic noneysir includes al1 bonds
and evidence of indebtedness, and all noneys belonging to the
state, or any city, county, tohrn, district, 
or public agency
therein, and aII Doneys, bonds, and evidences of indebtedness
received or heLd by state, county, district, 
city, town, or public
agency officers in their official capacity. (Penal Code Section
426.) Therefore, the scope of the anendment to Section 87200 cov-
ers all public agencies, including special districts.
We believe that the tern trother public officials 
who nanage
public investmentstt should be construed narror.rly. Thus,
individuals who would be included should be elected officers or


File No. I-9o-o72
Page 4
high-Ievel Public employees who manage public investments and
roeib"rs of Loards and conrnissions who manage public investuents '
Fron the facts provided, both you and Dr. Hadden perform a
statutory duty which- reguires the deposit of individual private
funds in-an iirsured account in a financial institution or with the
county treasurer of the county in which the proceedings are pend-
ing. 
(Probate code Section 7640(a).) Since neither you nor Dr'
gadden nanage rrpublic investrnents,tt you are not subject to the ne!'
disclosure iequirernents under amended section a72oo.
I trust this answers your questions. If you have any further
questions regarding this Datter, Please contact ne at (916)
322-590L.
Sincerely,
Kathryn E. Donovan
General counsel
By: Jil
Cou
Stecher
Lega1 Division
KED: JRS: PIh
R.
